body:
Automated sync of Dart CLI from Rust CLI changes.

**Source commit:** [`708415e`](https://github.com/breez/spark-sdk/commit/708415e71e2174b675503ab9612f3f399bf5610c)
**Claude log:** [View artifact](https://github.com/breez/spark-sdk/actions/runs/22794468076)


Sync findings

## Divergences
- Dart CLI missing passkey support entirely (--passkey, --wallet-name, --list-wallet-names, --store-wallet-name, --rpid flags and seed derivation logic)
- Dart README missing passkey documentation section and CLI options for passkey flags

## Applied
- Added `lib/passkey.dart` with `FilePrfProvider` (HMAC-SHA256 file-based PRF) and `resolvePasskeySeed()` orchestration using SDK `Passkey` class
- Updated `bin/breez_cli.dart` to add --passkey, --wallet-name, --list-wallet-names, --store-wallet-name, --rpid CLI flags with validation matching Rust clap constraints
- Updated `lib/cli.dart` to accept `PasskeyConfig` and use passkey seed derivation when --passkey is provided, falling back to mnemonic otherwise
- Updated `README.md` with passkey CLI options table and Passkey section documenting the file provider with usage examples

## Skipped
- YubiKey provider: requires USB HID access via `yubico-manager` crate (Rust). No well-known Dart package for YubiKey OTP challenge-response. Provider prints "not yet supported" error at runtime.
- FIDO2 provider: requires CTAP2 hmac-secret extension via `ctap-hid-fido2` crate (Rust) and system HID libraries. No well-known Dart package for CTAP2/FIDO2 HID transport. Provider prints "not yet supported" error at runtime.
- --rpid flag: accepted by the Dart CLI parser but only relevant to the FIDO2 provider which is not yet implemented. The flag is parsed and validated (requires --passkey) but not used.

title: chore: sync Go CLI with Rust CLI changes
body:
Automated sync of Go CLI from Rust CLI changes.

**Source commit:** [`708415e`](https://github.com/breez/spark-sdk/commit/708415e71e2174b675503ab9612f3f399bf5610c)
**Claude log:** [View artifact](https://github.com/breez/spark-sdk/actions/runs/22794468076)


Sync findings

## Divergences
- Passkey support (--passkey, --wallet-name, --list-wallet-names, --store-wallet-name, --rpid flags) missing from Go CLI entirely
- File-based PRF provider not implemented in Go CLI
- Passkey seed resolution orchestration (resolve_passkey_seed) not implemented in Go CLI
- lnurl-withdraw prompt does not show min/max amounts like Rust CLI
- lnurl-auth does not show confirmation prompt with domain/action before authenticating
- buy-bitcoin prints raw JSON instead of user-friendly "Open this URL" message
- claim-htlc-payment prints entire response instead of just the .payment field
- Go README missing passkey documentation section and CLI options

## Applied
- Added passkey.go with FilePrfProvider (HMAC-SHA256), stub providers for YubiKey/FIDO2, and resolvePasskeySeed orchestration
- Added --passkey, --wallet-name, --list-wallet-names, --store-wallet-name, --rpid flags to main.go
- Updated main.go seed resolution to conditionally use passkey or mnemonic
- Fixed lnurl-withdraw to show min/max withdrawal amounts in prompt (commands.go)
- Fixed lnurl-auth to show confirmation prompt with domain and action before authenticating (commands.go)
- Fixed buy-bitcoin to print "Open this URL in a browser" message instead of raw JSON (commands.go)
- Fixed claim-htlc-payment to print only result.Payment instead of entire response (commands.go)
- Updated README.md with passkey CLI options and passkey documentation section

## Skipped
- YubiKey PRF provider: Requires YubiKey HMAC-SHA1 challenge-response (Rust uses `challenge-response` crate / `yubico-manager`). No well-known Go equivalent for YubiKey OTP challenge-response was found. Implemented as a stub provider that returns "not yet supported" error.
- FIDO2 PRF provider: Requires CTAP2 hmac-secret extension (Rust uses `ctap-hid-fido2` crate). Go packages like `go-webauthn/webauthn` focus on server-side RP verification, not client-side CTAP2 HID transport. Implemented as a stub provider that returns "not yet supported" error. The `--rpid` flag is accepted but unused until a FIDO2 provider is implemented.

title: chore: sync Swift CLI with Rust CLI changes
body:
Automated sync of Swift CLI from Rust CLI changes.

**Source commit:** [`708415e`](https://github.com/breez/spark-sdk/commit/708415e71e2174b675503ab9612f3f399bf5610c)
**Claude log:** [View artifact](https://github.com/breez/spark-sdk/actions/runs/22794468076)


Sync findings

## Divergences
- Swift CLI missing passkey support entirely: no `--passkey`, `--wallet-name`, `--list-wallet-names`, `--store-wallet-name`, `--rpid` CLI flags
- Swift CLI missing passkey seed resolution orchestration (`resolvePasskeySeed` using SDK `Passkey` type)
- Swift CLI missing PRF provider implementations (file-based HMAC-SHA256, YubiKey skeleton, FIDO2 skeleton)
- Swift README missing passkey CLI options in the options table
- Swift README missing passkey documentation section

## Applied
- Created `Passkey.swift` with `FilePrfProvider` (full HMAC-SHA256 implementation using CommonCrypto), `YubiKeyPrfProvider` (skeleton), `Fido2PrfProvider` (skeleton), and `resolvePasskeySeed()` orchestration
- Updated `main.swift` to parse `--passkey`, `--wallet-name`, `--list-wallet-names`, `--store-wallet-name`, `--rpid` flags and resolve seed via passkey when `--passkey` is provided
- Updated `README.md` to add passkey CLI options to the options table and a Passkey section with file provider usage examples

title: Reserve leaves on prepare
body:
Closes #697 

## Summary

- Adds opt-in `reserve_leaves` flag to `PrepareSendPaymentRequest` that locks the selected UTXO-like leaves during prepare, ensuring the same leaves are used at send time. This prevents failures from concurrent payments consuming leaves between prepare and send, and avoids fee quote mismatches for Bitcoin address withdrawals (where different leaves could produce different on-chain fees).
- Adds `cancel_prepare_send_payment` to explicitly release reserved leaves when a payment is abandoned. Reservations auto-expire after 5 minutes.
- Extends `InMemoryTreeStore` and `PostgresTreeStore` with `get_reservation`, `swap_reservation`, and reservation expiry support.

## Notes on onchain withdrawal handling

Onchain withdrawals require two distinct sets of leaves: **amount leaves** (the value being withdrawn) and **fee leaves** (paying the on-chain coop-exit fee to the operator). The fee quote itself depends on which amount leaves are selected, because different leaf denominations produce different transaction structures and therefore different fees. This is the core reason reservations matter for onchain payments.

At prepare time, `fetch_coop_exit_fee_quote` selects leaves for the withdrawal amount, gets a fee quote based on those specific leaves, and — when `reserve_leaves` is true — keeps the reservation alive instead of releasing it. This locks the amount leaves so the fee quote stays valid.

At send time, fee leaves are handled differently depending on the fee policy:

- **FeesExcluded** (default): The reservation only covers the amount leaves. Fee leaves are selected separately at send time via a new `select_leaves_with_retry` call, since the fee is paid on top of the amount from unreserved balance.
- **FeesIncluded**: The reservation covers the full amount (fees come out of it). At send time, the reserved leaves are split into amount and fee subsets. If the leaf denominations don't allow a clean split, a swap is performed on the reservation to get the right denominations.
- **Withdraw all**: All reserved leaves are used as withdraw leaves; no separate fee leaves are needed (fee is deducted from the withdrawal output).

Both the amount reservation and any fee reservation are finalized on success or cancelled on failure.

title: Stable balance improvements
body:
- Runtime state switching - Stable balance activates/deactivates via user settings in real time without restart
- Event middleware - Adds middleware that can mutate/react/suppress events. Used to:
   - React and inject a conversion status to a succeeded payment, before queuing a per-receive conversion
   - Suppress payment events for conversion child payments (the internal send/receive plumbing), so external listeners only see the parent payment events
- Per-receive conversions - Each incoming payment is individually converted to the stable token on receipt, using deterministic transfer IDs for idempotency
- Reserved removed - Auto-convert now converts the full sats balance above threshold instead of holding back reserved_sats (with token dust checks)
- Dynamic min limit adjustment - Sends ToBitcoin automatically raise the amount to meet the min conversion limit when trying to send lower amounts, and convert the full token balance to avoid stranded token dust
- Token dust prevention - Auto-convert skips if converting would leave a token balance below the ToBitcoin min limit

  - UTXO syncing switched from per-address queries (`get_utxos_for_address`) to a single identity-based paginated query (`get_utxos_for_identity`), so all addresses are covered regardless of how many exist.

title: Swap leaves to ensure quoted leave amount does not change above limit
body:
Replaces #730. Closes #697 (resolving the underlying issue using a different strategy from what was suggested in the issue)

When an on-chain withdrawal fee quote is fetched, the SSP bases fees on the current leaf count. If leaves change between quoting and sending (e.g., due to incoming payments splitting leaves), the leaf count may exceed the SSP's tolerance, causing the withdrawal to fail.

  This PR:
  - Records the quoted leaf count when fetching a fee quote
  - Computes the SSP's max allowed leaf count using `ceil(q * (1.2 + 1.0 / q^0.6))`
  - Passes a `max_amount_leaf_count` constraint through leaf selection, forcing a consolidation swap if needed
  - Rejects the withdrawal if the swap still can't bring the count within tolerance
  - Adds an integration test covering this scenario

title: multi-instance lnurl server
body:
There were a few places where the LNURL server isn't great when it's run in a multi-instance scenario. This PR addresses those.
- The webhook secret is stored in the database. All instances now use the same webhook secret, so webhook registrations are really idempotent and don't affect other instances.
- When the newly_paid queue is being processed, instances claim the queue items first. That way there won't be multiple instances sending zap receipts for the same invoice.

fix serde_json arbitrary_precision breaking sync
body:
Due to https://github.com/serde-rs/json/issues/505 and https://github.com/serde-rs/serde/issues/1183, internally tagged enums can't be deserialized when they contain f64 values and `serde_json`'s `arbitrary_precision` feature is enabled. 

Sync was failing for Rust consumers who enabled `arbitrary_precision` in their projects (it's enabled globally). The `graphql_client` crate generates Rust enums with internally tagged fields, meaning they can be affected by this serde(_json) bug. 

The problematic f64 field was `preferred_currency_value_approx`, which we don't use, so for now we can prevent the issue by removing it.

title: Support unified Postgres backend
body:
This PR refactors token storage and introduces a Postgres implementation, aligning with the recent tree store fixes and addressing several edge cases.
During this work, I discovered a flaky integration test that exposed a real bug, fixed in commit: https://github.com/breez/spark-sdk/pull/748/changes/e9cd2cfeab6231370af72b7ae2a90703532d7c13
The issue was a race condition between swap_leaves and the background claimer when claiming transfers. If the background claimer won the race, the swap would fail. To resolve this, I added a delay in the background claimer for swap-related incoming transfers, giving the swap flow enough time to complete without interference.

Partially addresses #453

This makes use of the new `get_utxos_for_identity` method, which allows requesting not yet claimable utxos. For now, it only includes utxos that are missing enough confirmations to claim but have at least 1 confirmation, which is why it doesn't fully resolve #453. But it already helps that the SDK shows the unclaimed deposit after one confirmation instead of it being invisible until its claimable (3 confs).

No integration tests are included because on regtest, deposits are claimable with 1 confirmation, rendering testing on it impossible. Testing manually on mainnet with real funds is required.

- Add `NewDeposits` SDK event emitted when previously unknown deposits are detected during sync
- Request pending (unconfirmed) UTXOs from operators via `include_pending: true` in `GetUtxosForIdentity`

title: Set idempotency key in renew_leaf gRPC calls
body:
## Summary
- Adds `x-idempotency-key` gRPC metadata header to all three `renew_leaf` call sites (`renew_node`, `renew_refund`, `renew_zero_timelock`)
- Uses the node's refund transaction txid as the idempotency key, matching the approach in the [JS SDK](https://github.com/buildonspark/spark/blob/main/sdks/js/packages/spark-sdk/src/services/transfer.ts#L1822)
- Extracts a reusable `set_idempotency_key` helper in a new `metadata.rs` module for use by future RPC endpoints

## Motivation
We've been seeing `renew_leaf` failures when paying Spark invoices:
```
validating refresh timelock failed: leaf {leaf_id} refund transaction sequence must be less than or equal to 300, got 2000
```
Root cause: the SDK occasionally calls `renew_leaf` twice for the same leaf within milliseconds. The first call succeeds but the second fails because the leaf was already renewed. With the idempotency key, the signing operator returns the cached response from the first call instead of processing the duplicate.

title: Send all with stable balance
body:
Send-all with stable balance works by combining token conversion with `FeesIncluded` fee policy. The user provides their full token balance as the amount, sets a `token_identifier`, and specifies `ConversionOptions` with `ToBitcoin` conversion type along with `FeePolicy::FeesIncluded`. During prepare, the SDK estimates the total sats available by converting all tokens to sats and adding any existing sat balance, then deducts fees. At send time, the SDK re-evaluates whether this is still a send-all based on current balance, if so converts the full token amount using `AmountIn` (rather than `MinAmountOut`), then queries the actual post-conversion sat balance and sends the entire balance minus fees - effectively draining the wallet completely

**Breaking change:**
- PrepareLnurlPayRequest
  - Replaces `amount_sats` with `amount`
  - Adds `token_identifier` 

Closes #770

title: Expose webhook
body:
Summary                                                                                         
                                                                                                  
  - Notify external systems when an LNURL invoice is paid via a configurable per-domain webhook   
  - Webhook URLs are configured in the domain_webhooks table (manually populated)                 
  - The webhook delivery system (webhooks/ module) is generic and reusable for future webhook     
  types — it only knows about identifiers, URLs, and JSON payloads                                
  - Payload uses the { "template": "...", "data": { ... } } envelope, same as other Breez webhooks
  - amount_sat is nullable because the SSP webhook doesn't always include htlc_amount (e.g. older 
  SSP versions, or non-standard payment flows). Not ideal, but I didn't find a way around it.     
                                                                                                  
  Delivery guarantees                                                                             
                                                                                                  
  - Deliveries are persisted to the database before being sent, so nothing is lost on crash       
  - Failed deliveries are retried with exponential backoff (30s base, 1.5x multiplier, no cap)
  - Delivery records are kept for a configurable TTL (default 90 days) for debugging, then cleaned
   up hourly                                                                                      
                                                                                                  
  Throttling                                                                                      
                                                                                               
  - Per-URL concurrency limit (20 in-flight requests per URL)
  - The claim query returns at most one pending delivery per unique URL per poll cycle, so a
  slow/down domain cannot starve deliveries to other domains                                      
  - If a URL's concurrency limit is reached, excess deliveries are unclaimed and retried next
  cycle                                                                                           
                                                                                               
  Other changes                                                                                   
                                                                                               
  - Renamed newly_paid table to pending_zap_receipts to clarify its purpose                       
  - Moved zap receipt publishing logic from background.rs into zap.rs
  - Added domain and amount_received_sat columns to the invoices table                            
  - Parse htlc_amount from the SSP webhook payload 

Fixes #49

body:
Built-in PRF providers so integrators can enable passkey login with minimal platform-specific code.

## Summary
- Add ready-to-use PRF providers for Web, iOS/macOS, Android, React Native, Flutter, and Kotlin KMP
  - Web: standalone JS implementation using WebAuthn PRF extension
  - iOS/macOS and Android: native implementations using AuthenticationServices and Credential Manager respectively
  - React Native and Flutter: platform channel wrappers that delegate to the native iOS/Android implementations above
  - Kotlin KMP: repackages the Android Credential Manager implementation for KMP targets
- Default RP ID `keys.breez.technology` for cross-platform credential sharing
- Android 9+ via Play Services, Android 14+ with any compatible credential provider
- iOS 18+ / macOS 15+ with ObjC bridge
  - Apple marks PRF types as NS_REFINED_FOR_SWIFT on Xcode 26, requiring an ObjC helper to access them
- Fix Dart exception handling at flutter_rust_bridge FFI boundary
  - Dart exceptions now propagate as typed errors instead of causing panics

## Known issues
- Passkey support depends on the device's credential provider (e.g. Google Password Manager, Bitwarden). Devices without a compatible provider (e.g. GrapheneOS) may not support passkeys
- RP domain registration (assetlinks.json, apple-app-site-association) is currently managed manually. Contact the team to register your app

Add retry logic with exponential backoff to faucet requests
body:
## Summary
Add automatic retry logic with exponential backoff to faucet funding requests in both breez-sdk and spark-itest integration tests. This improves reliability when dealing with transient faucet failures.

## Key Changes
- Added `Duration` import for timeout handling
- Added `warn` to tracing imports for retry logging
- Implemented retry mechanism with up to 3 retries (4 total attempts)
- Exponential backoff strategy: 2^attempt seconds between retries (2s, 4s, 8s)
- Extracted core funding logic into new `try_fund_address()` private method
- Main `fund_address()` method now handles retry loop and returns last error if all attempts fail
- Added warning logs indicating retry attempts with backoff duration

## Implementation Details
- Retries use exponential backoff: `Duration::from_secs(2u64.pow(attempt))`
- Backoff only applied after first attempt (attempt > 0)
- Logs include attempt count and total retries for visibility
- Changes applied consistently to both `crates/breez-sdk/breez-itest/src/faucet.rs` and `crates/spark-itest/src/faucet.rs`

title: Add user agent support to rtsync and breez server clients
body:
## Summary
This PR adds support for custom User-Agent headers in gRPC client communications across both native and WebAssembly transports.

## Key Changes
- **Transport layer enhancements**:
  - Refactored `transport_wasm.rs` from a type alias to a proper `Transport` struct that wraps `tonic_web_wasm_client::Client` and implements `tower_service::Service`
  - Added User-Agent header injection in the WASM transport's `call` method
  - Updated `transport.rs` to accept and apply user agent configuration to the endpoint

- **Client API updates**:
  - Modified `GrpcClient::new()` to accept an optional `user_agent` parameter in both native and WASM implementations
  - Updated `BreezSyncerClient::new()` to accept and forward the user agent parameter to `GrpcClient`
  - Added `user_agent` field to `RealTimeSyncParams` struct

- **SDK integration**:
  - Updated `SdkBuilder` to pass the user agent to real-time sync initialization
  - Updated all test calls to `BreezSyncerClient::new()` to include the new user agent parameter

- **Dependencies**:
  - Added `http` and `tower-service` crate dependencies for WASM target to support the new Transport implementation

## Implementation Details
The WASM transport implementation uses a custom `Transport` struct that intercepts requests and injects the User-Agent header before delegating to the underlying `tonic_web_wasm_client::Client`. The native transport leverages tonic's built-in `user_agent()` endpoint configuration method. This approach maintains consistency across both platforms while respecting their respective capabilities.

title: fix: allow unsigned Spark invoices in address parsing
body:
## Summary
- Allow parsing Spark invoices without a signature, matching the behavior of Spark Signing Operators which treat signatures as optional
- When a signature is present, it is still verified as before
- This enables server-side invoice generation (e.g. on behalf of a customer) where the server has the customer's public key but not their signing key

## Context
The Spark SO `validateInvoiceFields` function ([source](https://github.com/lightsparkdev/spark/blob/main/spark/so/handler/tokens/internal_prepare_token_handler.go#L952-L956)) only verifies signatures when present:
```go
if decoded.SparkAddress.Signature != nil {
    err := common.VerifySparkAddressSignature(...)
}
```

The SDK should follow the same contract.

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo check -p spark` passes
- [x] `cargo test -p spark` — all tests pass
- [ ] Existing signed invoice flows still work (signature verified when present)
- [ ] Unsigned invoices can now be parsed and used as payment requests

title: Add unilateral exit support to the SDK
body:
## Summary

- Adds SDK support for preparing and executing a unilateral exit, including listing leaves, building the transaction tree, CPFP fee-bumping PSBTs, a sweep transaction, and comprehensive documentation

## Design decisions

- **Leaf inspection exposed via `list_leaves`** — lets developers inspect wallet leaves with a minimum value filter before deciding which ones to exit, so they can skip leaves that would cost more in fees than they hold
- **Fee calculation uses weight units throughout** — fees are computed as `ceil(fee_rate * package_weight / 4)` rather than converting to vbytes first, avoiding rounding errors that caused "min relay fee not met" rejections
- **Package-level fee rate** — CPFP child transaction fees cover the full parent+child package weight, since the parent transactions have zero fees (ephemeral anchors)
- **Tap tweak in the signer** — `sign_hash_schnorr_with_tweak` always applies the BIP341 tap_tweak because the secret key is only accessible inside the signer. The caller passes `Option` (`None` for key-path-only spends) matching the `Address::p2tr` API
- **Sweep transaction built by the SDK** — the SDK constructs and signs the sweep transaction internally using the leaf-derived keys, so the caller only needs to broadcast it. The caller is responsible for providing the destination address and for dust checks
- **CPFP change chains to the same address** — each CPFP fee-bump sends change back to the external UTXO's address, and the next CPFP spends from that change output. The remaining value after the last CPFP is the caller's to spend
- **CSV timelock exposed on the response** — each transaction pair includes `csv_timelock_blocks` so the caller knows which transactions require waiting and for how long, without having to parse raw transactions
- **Developers sign themselves** — Developers have to sign the returned PSBT themselves.

title: ship dSYMs with breez_sdk_sparkFFI.xcframework
body:
Xcode archive validation rejected apps linking the Swift package with
"The archive did not include a dSYM for the breez_sdk_sparkFFI.framework
with the UUIDs [...]" because the XCFramework was assembled by copying
raw dylibs into a committed framework skeleton with no debug symbols
anywhere in the bundle.

- Build the iOS/Darwin bindings with CARGO_PROFILE_RELEASE_DEBUG=full so
  the dylibs carry a debug map that dsymutil can consume.
- Run dsymutil per target and stage a breez_sdk_sparkFFI.framework.dSYM
  next to each dylib; strip -S the shipped dylib afterwards so LC_UUID
  is preserved but debug info is dropped from the binary.
- In the merge jobs, lipo the per-arch DWARF Mach-Os into a single
  universal dSYM for the ios-arm64_x86_64-simulator and
  macos-arm64_x86_64 slices.
- Download the three per-slice dSYM artifacts in publish-swift and
  embed them under /dSYMs/breez_sdk_sparkFFI.framework.dSYM, and
  advertise them via DebugSymbolsPath=dSYMs in the xcframework plist.
